message:
fix(init): use word-boundary matching for database pack auto-detection

The `detect_database_packs_from_deps` function previously used raw
substring matching (`content.contains(kw)`), causing false positives:
- "pg" matched "upgrading", "webpage", "package" — nearly every package.json
- "motor" could match "motorcycle" in comments
- Other short keywords had similar collision risks

Added `contains_word()` helper that checks keyword is bounded by
non-alphanumeric characters (or string boundaries). Also fixed dead
branch in TOML generation where last array element had unnecessary
trailing comma.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
